_comment: Recruit.log MongoDB Collection Definitions — 2 example documents per collection with inline schema notes

companies._comment: COMPANIES COLLECTION: Root entity representing a hiring company. Each company embeds its job_listings array, which in turn embeds requirements and type-specific details. This denormalizes the Project 1 tables: Company, Job_Listing, Internship, Full_Time, Listing_Requirement, and Skill into a single hierarchical document.
companies._comment_company_name: Natural key — unique identifier for the company (replaces surrogate company_id from SQL)
companies._comment_company_size: Enum: startup | mid | large | enterprise — categorical size bucket

job_listings._comment_listing: EMBEDDED JOB_LISTING: Each listing lives inside its parent company document. This avoids cross-collection joins when querying 'all listings for a company'. The listing_id is a local identifier carried over from the SQL migration.
job_listings.title: SWE Intern Summer 2026
job_listings._comment_role_type: Discriminator field: internship | full-time | co-op — determines which detail sub-document is populated
job_listings._comment_salary: For internships this is monthly pay; for full-time this is annual salary
job_listings.description: Google SWE internship for BS/MS students
job_listings._comment_listing_type: Subtype discriminator used to match the correct detail sub-document below

internship_details._comment: EMBEDDED INTERNSHIP SUBTYPE: Only populated when listing_type='internship'. Replaces the Internship table from Project 1. Null for full-time listings.

requirements._comment: EMBEDDED REQUIREMENT: Denormalizes the Listing_Requirement + Skill join from SQL. Each requirement references a skill by name (not by foreign key) and specifies whether it is required vs. preferred.
requirements._comment_skill_type: Enum: language | framework | tool | concept — categorizes the skill

job_listings._comment_listing: Full-time listing example — internship_details is null, full_time_details is populated instead
job_listings.title: SWE L3 New Grad

full_time_details._comment: EMBEDDED FULL_TIME SUBTYPE: Only populated when listing_type='full_time'. Replaces the Full_Time table from Project 1.

companies._comment: Second company example — demonstrates a company with one internship listing that has an empty requirements array and one full-time listing

job_listings._comment_listing: Full-time listing with an empty requirements array — not all listings specify skill requirements
job_listings.title: Production Engineer E3

applicants._comment: APPLICANTS COLLECTION: Root entity representing a student or job-seeker. Embeds skills (replacing Applicant_Skill + Skill join) and applications (replacing Application + its foreign keys to Applicant and Job_Listing). References to companies/listings are stored as denormalized name strings rather than ObjectId refs — this trades referential integrity for read performance and simpler queries.
applicants._comment_email: Natural key — unique identifier for the applicant
applicants._comment_is_anonymized: Business rule: all applicant data is anonymized by default (no real names stored). Advisors see aggregate data only unless is_anonymized=false.

skills._comment: EMBEDDED SKILL: Denormalizes the Applicant_Skill join table from SQL. Each skill includes the applicant's self-reported proficiency and experience duration.

applications._comment: EMBEDDED APPLICATION: Denormalizes the Application table from SQL. Instead of foreign keys to company_id and listing_id, we store company_name and listing_title as denormalized strings. This allows querying an applicant's full application history in a single document read.
applications._comment_status: Enum: applied | accepted | rejected | withdrawn — tracks current application state
applications._comment_rejection_stage: Populated only when status='rejected'. Enum: application | OA | phoneScreen | interview. Null otherwise. Supports business rule: rejections are tracked at specific pipeline stages.
applications._comment_is_active: Business rule: applications can be deactivated (e.g., after withdrawal) but not deleted

applications._comment: Example of a rejected application — rejection_stage captures where in the pipeline the rejection occurred
Example of a withdrawn application — is_active set to false

applicants._comment: Second applicant example — MS student with strong quant background and multiple offers

advisors._comment: ADVISORS COLLECTION: Root entity representing a faculty member, career counselor, or institutional researcher who monitors job listings. Embeds bookmarks (replacing the Advisor_Bookmark join table from SQL). Advisors do NOT see individual applicant data unless is_anonymized=false; they primarily interact with aggregate statistics and bookmarked listings.
advisors.name: Dr. Sarah Chen
advisors._comment_email: Natural key — unique identifier for the advisor
advisors._comment_role: Enum: faculty | careerCenter | institutionalResearcher | consultant — determines dashboard permissions and data visibility

bookmarks._comment: EMBEDDED BOOKMARK: Denormalizes the Advisor_Bookmark table from SQL. References a listing by company_name + listing_title (denormalized strings). Tracks when the advisor last viewed the listing and whether it is still actively bookmarked.
Example of an un-bookmarked entry — kept in history for analytics but is_bookmarked=false

advisors._comment: Second advisor example — institutional researcher role with limited bookmarks
advisors.name: James Rodriguez

_schema_design_notes.normalization_to_denormalization: Project 1 used 11 normalized SQL tables: Company, Job_Listing, Internship, Full_Time, Skill, Listing_Requirement, Applicant, Applicant_Skill, Application, Advisor, Advisor_Bookmark. Project 2 collapses these into 3 MongoDB collections by embedding child entities within their natural parents.
_schema_design_notes.embedding_rationale: Documents are structured around access patterns: (1) companies own listings and their requirements, (2) applicants own their skills and application history, (3) advisors own their bookmarks. Each pattern maps to a single-document read.
_schema_design_notes.cross_collection_references: Instead of ObjectId references, denormalized string fields (company_name, listing_title) link applications/bookmarks to listings. This trades write-time consistency for read-time simplicity — appropriate for an analytics-oriented read-heavy workload.
_schema_design_notes.subtype_pattern: The Internship/Full_Time specialization hierarchy uses a discriminator field (listing_type) with mutually exclusive sub-documents (internship_details / full_time_details), implementing MongoDB's polymorphic pattern.
